channel.title: Surviving the Singularity — Research Signals
channel.description: Algorithmically swept arXiv research ranked by singularity relevance. Not human-curated.

item.title: Automatic Hard Example Synthesis with Multi-Level Agentic Data Curation
item.description: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) are increasingly deployed for nuanced content safety and moderation tasks, yet they remain vulnerable to adversarial attacks and out-of-distribution edge cases. Traditional active learning and manual annotation fail to scale against the complexity and volume ... [Score: 12.0 | Flagged for: agi, agent, multi-agent, agentic, llm] Authors: Genglin Liu, Muye Zhang, et al.

item.title: Scaling Behavior Foundation Model for Humanoid Robots
item.description: Humanoid control requires natural whole-body coordination, precise real-time responses to control signals, and robust generalization across diverse environmental contexts, making it a cornerstone for generalist embodied agents. Behavior Foundation Models (BFMs) have recently emerged as a promising s... [Score: 11.5 | Flagged for: agi, foundation model, agent, embodied, humanoid] Authors: Weishuai Zeng, Kangning Yin, et al.

item.title: Digital Pantheon: Simulating and Auditing Coalition Formation with LLM Agents
item.description: The formation of political coalitions is a complex negotiation driven by both concrete policy objectives and deep-seated ideological convictions. While Large Language Models (LLMs) open new avenues for computational political science, the neutrality and helpfulness biases instilled by Reinforcement ... [Score: 11.0 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, alignment, rlhf, llm] Authors: Dylan Van Mulders, Matthias Bogaert, et al.

item.title: RetroAgent: Harnessing LLMs to Search Over Structured Memory for Agentic Retrosynthesis Planning
item.description: Multi-step retrosynthesis planning seeks to decompose a target molecule into commercially available building blocks through a sequence of feasible reactions. The vast combinatorial search space makes this task challenging even for expert chemists. Traditional methods combine tree search with offline... [Score: 10.0 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, planning, reasoning, llm] Authors: Yanqiao Zhu, Jingru Gan, et al.

item.title: Can Agentic Trading Systems Pay for Their Own Intelligence?
item.description: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly used in trading systems, where model reasoning, tool use, and continual decisions incur costs that are expected to produce trading value. Existing evaluations typically report performance metrics, but rarely examine agentic viability: whether dynami... [Score: 9.5 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, tool use, reasoning, llm] Authors: Qiqi Duan, Changlun Li, et al.

item.title: A hierarchical memory architecture overcomes context limits in long-horizon multi-agent computational modeling
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) demonstrate remarkable reasoning capabilities, yet their stateless architecture fundamentally limits deployment in long-horizon research workflows requiring multi-session continuity and quantitative rigor. Here we present Ensemble QSP, a multi-agent framework featuring a... [Score: 9.5 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, autonomous, reasoning, llm] Authors: Shivendra G. Tewari, Holly Kimko

item.title: Agentic evolution of physically constrained foundation models
item.description: Artificial intelligence increasingly drives automated scientific discovery, yet contemporary generalist agents lack physical grounding, frequently hallucinating hardware-incompatible designs. Here, we present a physically grounded, multi-agent discovery engine that autonomously architects hardware-c... [Score: 9.5 | Flagged for: foundation model, agent, multi-agent, agentic, autonomous] Authors: Jiangwei Zhang, Wen Sun, et al.

item.title: MARS: Multi-hop Adaptive Retrieval and SPARQL Generation for KGQA
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong reasoning performance, but their tendency to hallucinate limits their reliability in knowledge-intensive tasks requiring up-to-date and grounded information. Combining knowledge graphs (KGs) with LLMs facilitates the use of explicit symbolic know... [Score: 9.0 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, reasoning, llm, language model] Authors: Nikit Srivastava, Daniel Vollmers, et al.

item.title: OpenRCA 2.0: From Outcome Labels to Causal Process Supervision
item.description: Root cause analysis (RCA) poses a holistic test of LLM agentic capabilities, such as long-context understanding, multi-step reasoning, and tool use. However, existing datasets suffer from a fundamental gap: they label only the root cause, not the propagation path connecting it to the observed sympto... [Score: 9.0 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, tool use, reasoning, llm] Authors: Aoyang Fang, Yifan Yang, et al.

item.title: IDEA: Insensitive to Dynamics Mismatch via Effect Alignment for Sim-to-Real Transfer in Multi-Agent Control
item.description: Complex multi-agent control tasks remain challenging for traditional rule-based and model-based approaches, motivating the adoption of learning-based methods. However, learning-based methods often struggle with sim-to-real transfer because they rely on accurate dynamics modeling or system identifica... [Score: 9.0 | Flagged for: agi, agent, multi-agent, alignment, rag] Authors: Chenlong Liu, Zhuohui Zhang, et al.

item.title: SearchOS-V1: Towards Robust Open-Domain Information-Seeking Agent Collaboration
item.description: Recent advances in Tool-Integrated Large Language Models have made web search a core capability of information-seeking agents. However, as interaction histories grow, agents increasingly struggle to track task progress. When search attempts fail to yield useful evidence, current single- and multi-ag... [Score: 8.5 | Flagged for: agi, agent, multi-agent, language model, rag] Authors: Yuyao Zhang, Junjie Gao, et al.

item.title: BadWAM: When World-Action Models Dream Right but Act Wrong
item.description: World-action models (WAMs) are emerging as a promising foundation for embodied control: rather than predicting actions alone, they learn representations that couple action generation with future world prediction. This coupling is often viewed as a source of robustness, interpretability, and safety, ... [Score: 8.5 | Flagged for: agi, interpretability, interpret, embodied, robot] Authors: Qi Li, Xingyi Yang, et al.

item.title: The Dynamic Verifiable Multi-Agent Human Agentic Loyalty Loop (DVM-HALL) Model and the Net Human-Agent Score (NHAS) in Autonomous Commerce
item.description: The rapid proliferation of Agentic Artificial Intelligence fundamentally disrupts traditional customer loyalty paradigms. As AI evolves from passive recommendation algorithms to autonomous, goal-directed agents capable of executing purchasing decisions, the conventional understanding of consumer-bra... [Score: 8.5 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, agentic, autonomous, autonomy] Authors: Sai Srikanth Madugula, Peplluis Esteva de la Rosa, et al.

item.title: Can Induced Emotion Bias LLM Behaviors in Sequential Decision Making?
item.description: As Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as autonomous agents in high-stakes domains, understanding contextual factors that may modulate their decision-making becomes critical. While LLMs are trained to perceive and resonate with users' emotions, it remains unclear whether induced e... [Score: 8.5 | Flagged for: agi, agent, autonomous, llm, language model] Authors: Minh Khoi Ho, Zihao Zhu, et al.

item.title: Instance-Enriched Semantic Maps for Visual Language Navigation
item.description: Visual Language Navigation (VLN) aims to enable an embodied agent to navigate complex environments by following natural language instructions. Recent approaches build semantic spatial maps and leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) for reasoning and decision making. Despite these advances, existing s... [Score: 8.5 | Flagged for: agent, reasoning, embodied, llm, language model] Authors: Jiho Hong, Eunae Kang, et al.

item.title: Meta-Learning Preferences for Multilingual LLM Alignment
item.description: Unequal availability of human preference data across languages poses a significant challenge for aligning large language models in multilingual settings. To address the lack of sufficient data in low-resource language alignment, we propose a meta-learning framework for Reinforcement Learning from Hu... [Score: 8.5 | Flagged for: agi, alignment, llm, language model, rag] Authors: Jiaying Lin, Seongho Son, et al.

item.title: From World Action Models to Embodied Brains: A Roadmap for Open-World Physical Intelligence
item.description: Artificial general intelligence ultimately requires agents that can reason and act in the physical world. Action models, vision-language-action policies, and world models have advanced this goal, while World Action Models (WAMs) are particularly promising because they connect candidate interventions... [Score: 8.5 | Flagged for: artificial general intelligence, world model, agent, embodied, rag] Authors: Yuanzhi Liang, Xufeng Zhan, et al.

item.title: Agentic Knowledge Tracing: A Multi-Agent LLM Architecture for Stealth Assessment of Financial Literacy in Serious Games
item.description: Assessing financial literacy during gameplay without disrupting the learning experience remains a key challenge in serious games for education. We present the Agentic BKT pipeline, a multi-agent large language model architecture for stealth assessment of financial competencies from open-ended gamepl... [Score: 8.5 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, agentic, llm, language model] Authors: Gabriel Santos, Rita Julia, et al.

item.title: Show Me How You Reason and I'll Tell You Who You Are: Reasoning Graphs for Robust LLM Authorship Attribution
item.description: Given the current trend to employ large language models (LLMs) in almost any imaginable context, LLM-generated text detection and authorship attribution have become a pressing issue. Prior work has primarily focused on surface-level linguistic features, an approach shown to be susceptible to paraphr... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agi, reasoning, llm, language model, rag] Authors: Zlata Kikteva, Artur Romazanov, et al.

item.title: SafeRelBench: A Spatial-Relation-Aware Benchmark for Process-Level Safety in VLM-Driven Embodied Agents
item.description: Vision-language models (VLMs) are increasingly used as the reasoning backbone of embodied agents, enabling robots to interpret visual scenes, follow language instructions, and plan multi-step actions. In household environments, however, safety depends not only on recognizing objects, but also on how... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, reasoning, interpret, embodied, robot] Authors: Huaigang Yang, Ya Li, et al.

item.title: ReasFlow: Assisting Reasoning-Centric Scientific Discovery in Applied Mathematics via a Knowledge-Based Multi-Agent System
item.description: Recent advances in Large Language Models have fueled autonomous AI agents capable of tackling complex scientific tasks, yet existing automated research systems remain predominantly focused on empirically driven domains with quantitative benchmarks, leaving theory-driven discovery, particularly in ma... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, autonomous, reasoning, language model] Authors: Yutong He, Daibo Li, et al.

item.title: FormalAnalyticGeo: A Neural-Symbolic Based Framework for Multimodal Analytic Geometry Problem Generation
item.description: Math reasoning has achieved significant progress with the rapid advancement of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), however analytic geometry remains largely underexplored, primarily due to the scarcity of annotated samples. Existing diagram generation approaches struggle with analytic geometry... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agi, reasoning, llm, language model, rag] Authors: Ruoran Xu, Wending Gao, et al.

item.title: Agentic Routing: The Harness-Native Data Flywheel
item.description: Large language model agents are increasingly executed not by a single model call, but by an execution harness that manages observation, context, control, action, state, and verification. At the same time, frontier and open models are becoming structurally specialized: a model that is strong at code ... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, tool use, reasoning, language model] Authors: Xinchen Liu, Hang Zhou, et al.

item.title: Multi-Agent LLMs Fail to Explore Each Other
item.description: Exploration is essential for reliable autonomy in multi-agent systems, yet it remains unclear whether large language model (LLM) agents can explore effectively when interacting with one another. We show that modern LLM agents fail to do so, often exhibiting myopic and polarized interaction patterns ... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, autonomy, llm, language model] Authors: Hyeong Kyu Choi, Jiatong Li, et al.

item.title: PFAdapter: Hierarchical LoRA Decomposition for Personalized Federated MLLMs
item.description: Agentic AI systems are reshaping communications and networking by deploying autonomous intelligent agents capable of collaborative learning while maintaining data privacy at network edges. Within distributed network environments, Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) serve as cognitive engines fo... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, autonomous, llm, language model] Authors: Jing Liu, Kun Yang, et al.

item.title: Shared Selective Persistent Memory for Agentic LLM Systems
item.description: Agentic LLM systems that generate code through multi-turn tool use face a fundamental context problem: each session starts from zero, discarding the configuration choices, domain constraints, data schemas, and tool-use patterns that made previous sessions productive. Naively persisting entire conver... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, tool use, tool-use, llm] Authors: Sanjana Pedada, Aditya Dhavala, et al.

item.title: Communication-Efficient Digital-Twin Coordination for Heterogeneous LLM Embodied Agents over Computing Power Networks
item.description: Embodied agent teams powered by heterogeneous large language models (LLMs) are being widely deployed in physical artificial intelligence such as smart factories, warehouses, and service robotics. To enable collaboration among such an agent team, efficient coordination mechanisms that operate reliabl... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, embodied, robot, robotics, llm] Authors: Nuocheng Yang, Sihua Wang, et al.

item.title: Who Broke the System? Failure Localization in LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems
item.description: Large language model (LLM) based multi-agent systems enable complex problem solving through coordinated reasoning and action, but their distributed structure also introduces new challenges in diagnosing system-level failures. When an execution fails, identifying which agent is responsible and at wha... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, reasoning, llm, language model] Authors: Yufei Xia, Anjun Gao, et al.

item.title: Information Limits and Attractor Dynamics in Economies of Frontier LLM Agents: A Pre-Registered Test
item.description: We report a pre-registered, two-part experiment on small economies of frontier language-model agents (Claude Opus 4.8), testing two quantitative predictions about coupled multi-agent systems: an information-theoretic capacity region for wealth growth under market coupling, and a mean-field residual-... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, alignment, llm, scaling] Authors: Cheng Qian

item.title: The Red Queen Gödel Machine: Co-Evolving Agents and Their Evaluators
item.description: Self-improving agents are state-of-the-art (SOTA) on agentic coding benchmarks and have recently been extended to general domains. However, their search methods generally assume a stationary evaluation criterion: a fixed verifier, benchmark, or labeled dataset that remains valid as the agent improve... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: recursive self-improvement, self-improving, agent, agentic] Authors: Alex Iacob, Andrej Jovanović, et al.
